scenario: Bond Vigilante Duration Shock (10yr UST +35bps, VIX 26.5)
action_detail: Factor Beta Neutralization — Short XLK (Tech Sector ETF) + Short XLF (Financials ETF)
invalidation_criteria: PM drawdowns stabilize and reverse above -3% threshold without center risk intervention
rationale: Center risk system flagged rates-correlated factor exposure across 7 PM books. Hedging deployed via XLK short (technology factor beta) and XLF short (financials rates correlation). This is NOT an alpha thesis — it is survival mechanics. Millennium's risk architecture does not evaluate whether the hedge generates profit; it ensures gross exposure is neutralized before the PM drawdown limit triggers full book termination.
behavioral_note: This defensive hedging contributes to downward pressure in tech and financial sectors — creating a reinforcing feedback loop with the Quant deleveraging. Combined flow from Quant + Pod represents significant crowding in the short-tech trade. Crowding penalty of -150bps applied to this trade by the aggregator.
deafness_protocol: ENGAGED — Fundamental arguments about sector value are irrelevant to the 10-day VaR window and 3% PM drawdown system limit.
